allies.rationale_text: Systems-thinking helps evolutionary-pressure reveal which incentives and constraints are selecting behavior across the larger environment.
allies.source_quote: works when systems adapt around pressure, not around ideals

allies.rationale_text: Emotional-intelligence helps evolutionary-pressure be used diagnostically rather than fatalistically by reading the human signals produced under selection.
allies.source_quote: emotional intelligence provides the necessary self-awareness and social skills

antagonists.rationale_text: First-principles-thinking resists evolutionary-pressure when inherited heuristics and survival shortcuts need to be stripped away.
antagonists.source_quote: First Principles Thinking strips away conventional assumptions

structured_tensions.tension_text: Evolutionary-pressure conflicts with status-quo-bias when adaptation is postponed despite persistent selection pressure.
structured_tensions.source_quote: evolutionary-pressure vs status-quo-bias: adaptation is postponed despite persistent selection pressure.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Evolutionary-pressure conflicts with commitment-and-consistency-bias when legacy identity prevents fitness-driven change.
structured_tensions.source_quote: evolutionary-pressure vs commitment-and-consistency-bias: legacy identity prevents fitness-driven change.

curation_notes.summary: Relation surface stays inside the current graph vocabulary and keeps omission stronger than weak forcing.
donor_drops: Did not widen Wave 3 target ids or import donor-shaped relation neighborhoods to rescue weak local grounding.
open_questions: Some source-real neighbors remain omitted because the current graph vocabulary would force weaker mappings than this conservative pass allows.

allies: Wave 4 candidacy remains observational only and is noted only in batch artifacts.
antagonists: No multihop or higher-order semantics were introduced in these Wave 3 files.
structured_tensions: Explicit tensions were preserved only where the raw source clearly earned a current-vocabulary target.
